definitions.technical: The amount of reserve assets held at one financial institution. Circle's $3.3 billion at SVB (roughly 8% of USDC's total reserves at the time) was sufficient to trigger a depeg and cascading DeFi liquidations.
definitions.lay: How much of a stablecoin issuer's reserves are stored at a single bank. Circle had about 8% of its total USDC reserves at Silicon Valley Bank, and that concentration alone was enough to cause a crisis when the bank failed.
definitions.parenthetical: (the share of total reserves held at a single custodian institution, where even small concentrations can trigger systemic events)
definitions.legal_significance: The SVB episode demonstrated that even a relatively small single-custodian concentration (8%) can trigger a depeg and cascading liquidations. This finding supports the comment letter's recommendation for mandatory concentration caps under Option B.
